findings.title: Stored XSS: command value and item label/value rendered as unescaped HTML text
findings.description: CommandValue renders `{commandValueText(props)}` (L369) and CommandItem renders `{props.children ?? props.itemLabel ?? props.itemValue}` (L316) as element text children. `commandValueText` (packages/headless-ui/src/primitives/command.ts:299-303) returns `selected.label ?? selected.textValue ?? selected.value` or `state.value` â€” i.e. data drawn from `props.items`/`props.value`. The @jiso/server JSX runtime does NOT escape text children: `renderJsxChildren` (packages/server/src/jsx-runtime.ts:79-84) returns `String(children)` verbatim (only attributes are escaped, jsx-runtime.ts:66). Command palettes are routinely populated from DB/user data (file names, product titles, search results). When a mutation/route renders this component into a wire fragment, the runtime applies it via `innerHTML`/`insertAdjacentHTML` (packages/runtime/src/response-fragment-apply.ts:58,62), so an item label like `` executes. This is the 'unescaped fragment HTML / stored XSS' risk called out in the project threat model. NOTE: the scanner's insecure-crypto flags on L51/64/206/212/232/240/243 are false positives â€” they match the substring 'des' in descriptionId/aria-describedby/aria-activedescendant, not a DES cipher.
findings.recommendation: Escape scalar text values before interpolating: render `{escapeHtml(commandValueText(props))}` and `{escapeHtml(props.itemLabel ?? props.itemValue)}` (escapeHtml is exported from @jiso/server, see packages/server/src/html.ts). Better, fix the root cause: make `renderJsxChildren` escape string children by default and provide an explicit raw-HTML/`children`-is-prerendered opt-in, since today every component that emits text is a latent XSS sink.
